title: Chapter 24: Theory & Practice of Premium Rating

cards.title: Pricing Fundamentals
cards.content:
• Pure Premium vs. Gross Premium
• Tariff vs. Detariffing (Market Rules)
• Motor Rating Factors (IDV, CC)
• No Claim Bonus (NCB) Rules

cards.title: 1. Pure vs Gross Premium
cards.content:
Pure Premium (Burning Cost): Only covers expected claims.
Gross Premium: Pure Premium + Expenses + Profit.

cards.title: 2. Third Party Rule
cards.content: CRITICAL: Premium for 'Motor Third Party Liability' is NOT free. It is strictly fixed by IRDAI annually.

cards.title: 3. Rating Factors
cards.content: Premium depends on: Cubic Capacity (CC), Age of vehicle, Zone, and IDV (Insured Declared Value).

cards.title: 4. Owner's Reward
cards.content: NCB follows the OWNER, not the vehicle. Can be transferred to a new car if the old one is sold.

cards.title: 5. Max Discount
cards.content: NCB can go up to a maximum of 50% discount in motor insurance.

sections.title: 🚀 Exam Pro-Tips (Repeated Themes)
items: NCB Owner: The Insured (not the car).
Own Damage factors: CC, IDV, Age, Zone.

cards.title: Q1. Who fixes Motor TP rates?
Q2. What happens to NCB if car is sold?
cards.content: Answer: Belongs to policyholder; can be transferred to new car.
